question: Two cars reach an intersection with no signs or signals at the same time. Who must yield?
choices: The driver on the left yields to the driver on the right
The driver on the right yields to the driver on the left
The faster vehicle goes first
Both must stop and wave each other through
explanation: At an intersection with no stop sign or signal, drivers must yield to vehicles approaching from the right.
citation: AR Driver License Study Guide, p. 39 — Right of Way at Stop Signs

question: You are turning left at an intersection with a green light. Oncoming traffic is approaching. You must:
choices: Turn quickly before the oncoming cars arrive
Yield to the oncoming cars traveling straight ahead and turn only when the gap is safe
Stop in the intersection and wait for the light to turn red
Honk to claim the right-of-way
explanation: Drivers turning left must yield to oncoming cars traveling straight ahead. When two vehicles face each other and one is turning left, the one going straight goes first.
citation: AR Driver License Study Guide, p. 35, 39 — Right of Way (Left Turns)

question: At a 4-WAY or ALL-WAY stop, who goes first?
choices: The driver who reached the intersection first, after making a complete stop
The car on the busiest road
The largest vehicle
The car turning left
explanation: First to stop = first to go. If two arrive at the same time, the vehicle on the right goes first. And when in doubt, let the other traffic go ahead — your safety comes first.
citation: AR Driver License Study Guide, p. 39-40 — Right of Way at Stop Signs

question: An ambulance approaches with lights and siren on while you are in an intersection. You should:
choices: Stop immediately where you are
Drive through the intersection first, then pull to the right edge of the road and stop
Speed up to get away from it
Move to the left lane
explanation: Pull to the right edge of the road and stop for emergency vehicles using lights/siren — but if you're in an intersection, go ahead and drive through it before pulling over.
citation: AR Driver License Study Guide, p. 11 — Move Over Law / Emergency Vehicles

question: You are entering a road from a driveway, alley, or parking lot. Who has the right-of-way?
choices: You, because you are entering traffic
Pedestrians on the sidewalk AND all vehicles already on the main road
Whoever is moving faster
Vehicles on the road, but not pedestrians
explanation: Drivers crossing a sidewalk from a driveway, alley, or parking lot must yield to pedestrians, and drivers entering a road must yield to vehicles already on it.
citation: AR Driver License Study Guide, p. 40 — General Right of Way Rules

question: A pedestrian is crossing in a crosswalk. You must:
choices: Slow down and steer around them
Honk so they hurry across
Yield — a driver must yield to pedestrians in or about to enter a crosswalk
Proceed if they are still on the other half of the street
explanation: Drivers must yield to pedestrians in or about to enter a crosswalk — and not all crosswalks are marked. Watch for pedestrians at every intersection.
citation: AR Driver License Study Guide, p. 34-35 — Crosswalks / Pedestrians

question: When entering a roundabout, you must:
choices: Yield to traffic in ALL lanes of the roundabout, not just the lane closest to you
Stop completely before entering, always
Enter quickly — circulating traffic yields to you
Yield only to traffic entering from your right
explanation: Slow down, and yield to traffic already circulating — in all lanes of the roundabout. Traffic travels counterclockwise around the center island. Arkansas has over 170 roundabouts.
citation: AR Driver License Study Guide, p. 36-38 — Roundabouts

question: Even with a green light, you should not start across an intersection unless:
choices: You can get through it without having to stop and block traffic
The driver behind you honks
You are turning right
Traffic is heavy
explanation: Drivers may not enter an intersection unless they can get through without stopping. Getting caught when the light changes blocks cross traffic — and you can be ticketed for blocking an intersection.
citation: AR Driver License Study Guide, p. 40, 61 — General Right of Way / Intersecting Traffic

question: A pedestrian carrying a white cane or using a guide dog is crossing ahead. They have:
choices: The same right-of-way as any pedestrian
Absolute right-of-way — and do not use your horn, which could confuse or frighten them
Right-of-way only in marked crosswalks
No special right-of-way
explanation: Blind pedestrians using a guide dog or white cane have ABSOLUTE right-of-way. Never honk at them — it can confuse or frighten a blind pedestrian.
citation: AR Driver License Study Guide, p. 35 — Pedestrians

question: You are passing another vehicle when it slows down ahead of you. Who must yield?
choices: You — the passing driver must yield to the vehicle being passed
The vehicle being passed must speed up
Neither vehicle
The vehicle being passed must pull onto the shoulder
explanation: Drivers passing a vehicle going the same direction must yield to the vehicle being passed — even if it slows or stops, since it may be turning left or avoiding a hazard you can't see.
citation: AR Driver License Study Guide, p. 40 — General Right of Way Rules (Passing)

question: Driving on a sidewalk is:
choices: Allowed when traffic is heavy
Illegal, except to cross it — and you must yield to pedestrians when crossing
Allowed for parking
Allowed for motorcycles only
explanation: It is illegal to drive on a sidewalk except to cross it (at a driveway or alley), and pedestrians on the sidewalk always have the right-of-way.
citation: AR Driver License Study Guide, p. 40 — General Right of Way Rules

question: In a two-lane roundabout, to turn LEFT you should generally:
choices: Use the left lane, unless signs or markings say otherwise
Use the right lane
Use either lane
Avoid roundabouts when turning left
explanation: Like other intersections: left lane to go left, right lane to go right, either lane to go straight unless marked otherwise. Yield to traffic in ALL lanes before entering, and give trucks extra room inside.
citation: AR Driver License Study Guide, p. 38 — How to Navigate Roundabouts

question: An oncoming car has its turn signal on. You should turn in front of it only when:
choices: You see its signal
The driver actually starts the turn — signals can be left on by mistake, and motorcycle signals don't cancel automatically
It is more than 100 feet away
You are at a 4-way stop
explanation: Don't trust a blinker alone — the driver may mean a turn beyond you or forgot to cancel the signal. Wait until they actually begin turning.
citation: AR Driver License Study Guide, p. 61 — Intersecting Traffic

question: Right-of-way rules give a driver:
choices: Absolute right-of-way when followed
No absolute right-of-way — you must still do everything possible to avoid striking a pedestrian or vehicle
Priority over pedestrians
The right to proceed without looking
explanation: Traffic law determines who must YIELD — it never grants absolute right-of-way. When in doubt, let the other traffic go ahead; your safety comes first.
citation: AR Driver License Study Guide, p. 35, 39 — Right of Way
